notes: Send 5 starting villagers to sheep and 1 to stone. Byzantines need early stone for Cisterns, so do not skip this.
Scout for sheep, gold, and your opponent.
Put new villagers onto wood, but keep 2 on stone until your first 50 stone is banked.
Build a House, then plan a tight base layout so the first Cistern boosts the Town Center and drop-offs.
Keep most villagers on food and add a Mill on berries.
The stone villagers are paying for your Cistern network; move them only after the first Cistern is affordable.
Send 3 villagers to gold with a Mining Camp.
With about 400 food and 200 wood, age up to Feudal. Keep the stone line running for the first Cistern.
Keep gathering through the age-up and make sure 50 stone is ready for your first Cistern.
Decide which matchup weakness a mercenary contract should cover once you hit Feudal.
In Feudal, build the first Cistern in the middle of your base to boost the economy around it.
Make Limitanei (tanky spearmen) to hold ground cheaply while you scale.
Use a Mercenary contract to hire units that patch your weakest matchup spot.
Keep 1 to 2 villagers on stone if you want a second Cistern soon; otherwise move them to food or gold.
Add stone again before expanding the Cistern network; broken or missing Cisterns slow the whole civ.
Bank toward Castle Age for your power units.
In Castle Age, add Cataphracts backed by mercenaries and siege.
Convert your economic lead into map control with Cataphract pushes, and keep the Cistern network alive.
